questions.text: I feel that there is nothing wrong with making myself the center of my own life.
I usually wait for someone else to propose a plan before weighing in.
I hold the view that my ambitions are a source of vitality and meaning.
I maintain detailed records and notes for projects I'm involved in.
I find that I intuitively map social networks, understanding who influences whom.
I often miss hints and wish people would just be direct with me.
I find that strict timelines stifle my creativity more than they help it.
I believe that diminishing my own visibility allows others to shine.
I believe that the moral weight of suffering is the ethical truth that should temper every exercise of authority and every allocation of advantage.
I think that the ability to hold suffering at an analytical distance is what makes it possible to respond to it systematically rather than haphazardly.
I believe that the ability to maintain a cool, logical perspective when confronted with suffering is a form of competence that the world needs more of.
I tend to frame requests as expectations rather than options.
I find social situations most comfortable when I know exactly what is expected of me.
I believe my even temperament is one of my most reliable qualities.
I rarely take notes, preferring to trust my memory and instincts.
I hold the view that emotional events are data points, and I process them without being swept away.
I hold the view that my nervous system is exceptionally sensitive to stimulation.
I am the type who helps the group discover its own rules of engagement.
